Ukraine Conflict News





03 February 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Chinese FM addresses question of what role China is playing in ensuring release of President Maduro and his wife Global Times 03 Feb 2026 -- Addressing the questions that since it has been one month since the US struck Venezuela and forcibly seized President Nicolás Maduro and his wife, how China assesses the current development, and what role China is playing in ensuring the release of Maduro and his wife, Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said that one month ago, the US brazenly carried out military strikes against Venezuela and forcibly seized President Maduro and his wife. Such hegemonic actions severely violate international law, infringe on Venezuela's sovereignty, and threaten peace and stability in Latin America and the Caribbean. China firmly opposes this.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Russia warns of military measures if US deploys weapons in Greenland Press TV 03 Feb 2026 -- Russia has warned it will take military countermeasures if the United States moves ahead with its controversial plans to deploy weapons in Greenland.

  • War Department Announces Vendors Invited to Compete in Phase I of the Drone Dominance Program DoD 03 Feb 2026 -- When Secretary of War Pete Hegseth took office a year ago, matching new technologies to threats was one of his core priorities. He commissioned an acquisition strategy to swiftly outfit our combat units with lethal drones.
  • MARCENT Hosts Lebanese Armed Forces for Security Summit CENTCOM 03 Feb 2026 -- U.S. Marine Corps Forces, Central Command (MARCENT) hosted a bi-lateral security summit attended by senior leaders from the Lebanese Armed Forces (LAF) at MacDill Air Force Base in Tampa, Fla., Jan. 29-30.
  • USS Greeneville Returns Home Following Deployment US Navy 03 Feb 2026 -- Los Angeles-class fast-attack submarine USS Greeneville (SSN 772) returned to its homeport of Naval Base Point Loma, following a regularly scheduled deployment to the Indo-Pacific region in support of U.S. national security objectives, January 30, 2026.

  • Ministry of Defence of Russian Federation holds regular meeting of Russian-Cambodian workgroup on military cooperation Russia MoD 03 Feb 2026 -- Today, the Ministry of Defence of the Russian Federation held a regular meeting of the Russian-Cambodian workgroup on military cooperation.

  • Estonia prepares a new assistance package for Ukraine, encompassing drones and counter-drone capabilities Ukraine MOD 03 Feb 2026
  • January saw the highest monthly use of guided aerial bombs by russian aviation since the start of the war Ukraine MOD 03 Feb 2026
  • Automatic extension now applies to 90% of deferments for Ukrainians Ukraine MOD 03 Feb 2026
  • Air defence and PURL contributions: Ukraine, NATO, the United Kingdom and Germany coordinate support priorities ahead of the Ramstein-format meeting Ukraine MOD 03 Feb 2026
  • Sweden and Denmark bolster Ukrainian air defence with modern anti-aircraft systems Government Offices of Sweden 03 Feb 2026 -- Minister for Defence Pål Jonson and Danish Minister for Defence Troels Lund Poulsen has presented a Swedish and Danish joint procurement of TRIDON Mk2 air defence systems to support Ukraine.
  • NATO Secretary General in Kyiv - NATO stands with Ukraine NATO 03 Feb 2026 -- On Tuesday 3 February 2026 NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte visited Kyiv, Ukraine for meetings with government leaders, including President Zelenskyy.
  • Rutte Says Foreign Troops Will Be Deployed to Ukraine Right After Conclusion of Peace Sputnik 03 Feb 2026 -- Foreign troops will appear in Ukraine immediately after the conclusion of a peace agreement, NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said on Tuesday.

  • Modernization of Russian Nuclear Triad Is at Advanced Stage - Deputy Foreign Minister Sputnik 03 Feb 2026 -- Modernization of the Russian nuclear triad is at an advanced stage, Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Sergey Ryabkov said on Tuesday.
  • Russia's New START Offer Still 'On The Table,' But No US Response Yet - Kremlin Sputnik 03 Feb 2026 -- Russia's initiative on the New START (New Strategic Arms Reduction Treaty) is still "on the table," but Moscow has not yet received any response from the United States, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said on Tuesday.
  • China Hopes for Positive US Response to Russia's New START Offer - Report Sputnik 03 Feb 2026 -- China has taken note of the constructive proposal of Russia on the New START (New Strategic Arms Reduction Treaty) and hopes that the United States will respond positively, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesman Lin Jian said on Tuesday.
  • China, Russia hold new round of strategic stability consultation as New START Treaty is expiring; Beijing, Moscow show prudent and responsible approach to arms control: Chinese expert Global Times 03 Feb 2026 -- Chinese Assistant Foreign Minister Liu Bin and Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Sergey Ryabkov on Tuesday co-chaired a new round of China-Russia strategic stability consultation in Beijing. The two sides held in-depth exchanges of views on the current global strategic stability situation and multilateral arms control issues, and reached broad consensus, according to a readout released by the Chinese Foreign Ministry.
  • Neither fair nor reasonable to ask China to join nuclear disarmament negotiations at this stage: Chinese FM on Trump's reported call to include China in nuclear arms control talks Global Times 03 Feb 2026 -- "China has noted the constructive proposals previously put forward by Russia regarding follow-up arrangements to the New START Treaty and hopes the US will respond positively and genuinely uphold global strategic stability," Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said on Tuesday in response to a Russian media inquiry about how China views the impact on global strategic stability in regard of the absence of any arms control treaty between the world's two largest nuclear powers.

  • Multiple countries reportedly interested in JF-17; Pakistan's questioned production capacity a manageable 'sweet problem' with China's support: Chinese expert Global Times 03 Feb 2026 -- Amid Pakistan's remarks that multiple countries have expressed interest in acquiring the JF-17 fighter jets, a Bloomberg report on Tuesday questioned whether Pakistan's production capacity of the China-Pakistan jointly developed aircraft can meet a potential sudden surge in demand. A Chinese aviation expert said that this could be a "sweet problem" for Pakistan, as backed by strong order demand, the country's aviation industry is expected to develop rapidly, and potential capacity constraints are ultimately not a true concern given China's support for Pakistan in manufacturing this type of fighter jet.
  • Commander Azerbaijan Naval Forces (ANF), Rear Admiral Shahin Mammadov called on Admiral Naveed Ashraf, NI, NI (M), T Bt, Chief of the Naval Staff, at Naval Headquarters, Islamabad. ISPR Pakistan 03 Feb 2026
  • Deputy Commander-in-Chief of the Libyan Arab Armed Forces, Lieutenant General Saddam Khalifa Haftar, called on Admiral Naveed Ashraf, NI, NI (M), T Bt, Chief of the Naval Staff, at Naval Headquarters, Islamabad. ISPR Pakistan 03 Feb 2026

  • Navy ANZAC Class frigates depart on Australia's first Regional Presence Deployments for 2026 Australia DOD 03 Feb 2026 -- Two Royal Australian Navy (RAN) Anzac Class frigates have sailed from Fleet Base West in Western Australia as part of the Australian Defence Force's Regional Presence Deployment program.

  • India and Tanzania Hold 4th Joint Defence Cooperation Committee Meeting in Zanzibar India PIB 03 Feb 2026 -- The fourth edition of the Joint Defence Cooperation Committee (JDCC) meeting between India and Tanzania was held in Zanzibar from 02-03 February 2026
  • DRDO carries out successful demonstration of Solid Fuel Ducted Ramjet technology India PIB 03 Feb 2026 -- Defence Research & Development Organisation (DRDO) has carried out successful demonstration of Solid Fuel Ducted Ramjet (SFDR) technology from the Integrated Test Range (ITR), Chandipur off the coast of Odisha at around 1045 hrs on February 03, 2026

  • Israeli, U.S. navies hold joint drill in Red Sea People's Daily 03 Feb 2026 -- Israeli and U.S. naval forces held a joint drill in the Red Sea on Sunday, the Israeli military said Monday, highlighting strengthened maritime cooperation as regional tensions with Iran continue to rise.

  • Defense minister underlines synergy in space field IRNA 03 Feb 2026 -- Defense minister Brigadier General Aziz Nasirzadeh has stressed independence in the field of space and creating synergy in this field.

  • Signing of the Agreement between the Government of Japan and the Government of the People's Republic of Bangladesh concerning the Transfer of Defense Equipment and Technology Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 03 Feb 2026
  • The Fifth Japan-Jordan Politico-Military (PM) Dialogue Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 03 Feb 2026

  • Kingdom of Saudi Arabia - F-15 Sustainment DSCA 03 Feb 2026 -- The State Department has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia of F-15 Sustainment and related equipment for an estimated cost of $3.0 billion. The Defense Security Cooperation Agency delivered the required certification notifying Congress.

  • The Prime Minister receives delegation led by Chief of Defence Staff of the Indian Armed Forces Prime Minister of the Republic of Armenia 03 Feb 2026 -- Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan received a delegation led by Chief of Defence Staff of the Indian Armed Forces, General Anil Chauhan, who is on an official visit to the Republic of Armenia.

  • Foreign Minister Tsahkna: continued US security assistance to the Baltic States strengthens NATO's deterrence and defence posture on the Alliance's eastern flank Estonia MFA 03 Feb 2026 -- Today, 3 February, the US Congress approved the US defence budget and the budget for overseas missions, which also include security assistance for the Baltic States.

  • Defence Minister's meeting with the Ambassador of the Kingdom of Norway to Romania Romania - Ministry of National Defence 03 Feb 2026 -- On Tuesday, February 3rd, Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, had a meeting, at the Ministry of National Defence headquarters, with the Ambassador of the Kingdom of Norway to Romania, Her Excellency Hilde Svartdal Lunde.
  • The Chief of Defence Staff has paid a visit to the Republic of Portugal Romania - Ministry of National Defence 03 Feb 2026 -- The Chief of Defence Staff, General Gheorghiță Vlad, has recently paid an official visit to Portugal, at the invitation of his counterpart, General José Nunes da Fonseca, Chief of Defence of the Portuguese Armed Forces.

  • National plan will strengthen resilience of critical entities Finnish Government 03 Feb 2026 -- The Ministry of the Interior is preparing a national plan that will form the basis for identifying critical entities and guide resilience development in all sectors.

  • REACT II Contract Signed OCCAR 03 Feb 2026 -- 3 February 2026, the OCCAR‑EA Director, Joachim Sucker, and the INDRA signee, Mr. Jesús Hortal Escolano, acting under a Power of Attorney granted by the other members of the REACT II Consortium, signed the Procurement Contract for the second stage of the Responsive Electronic Attack for Cooperative Tasks (REACT II) Programme.

  • Vietnam preparing for possible US military aggression: Leaked report Press TV 03 Feb 2026 -- A leaked internal document shows Vietnam is preparing, politically and militarily, for a potential US invasion, casting Washington as a threat to the country's sovereignty rather than a reliable partner.

  • PM call with President Trump of the United States: 3 February 2026 Gov.UK 03 Feb 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Republic of Korea Foreign Minister Cho US Dept. of State 03 Feb 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Indian External Affairs Minister Jaishankar US Dept. of State 03 Feb 2026
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Sierra Leonean Foreign Minister Kabba US Dept. of State 03 Feb 2026
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Argentine Foreign Minister Quirno US Dept. of State 03 Feb 2026
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Foreign Minister Sommerfeld US Dept. of State 03 Feb 2026

  • Senate Foreign Relations Democrats Statement on Reports of Trump Family Bribe from UAE Officials US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 03 Feb 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen, Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Chris Coons (D-DE), Chris Murphy (D-CT), Tim Kaine (D-VA), Jeff Merkley (D-OR), Cory Booker (D-NJ), Brian Schatz (D-HI),Chris Van Hollen (D-MD),Tammy Duckworth (D-IL)and Jacky Rosen (D-NV) released the following statement in response to reporting that a company backed by a senior United Arab Emirates official secretly purchased a major stake in a Trump-family cryptocurrency venture days before President Trump's Inauguration and just months before the Administration approved the sale of advanced U.S. artificial intelligence chips for the UAE.

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 3 February 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 03 Feb 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Prime Minister's Office Announcement Israel - Prime Minister 03 Feb 2026 -- The meeting between Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Steve Witkoff, the US President's Special Envoy, has concluded.
  • Statement by the Prime Minister's Office Israel - Prime Minister 03 Feb 2026 -- "The meeting between Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Steve Witkoff, the US President's Special Envoy, has concluded."

  • 1,900 attacks recorded in January as Israeli violence intensifies in occupied West Bank Press TV 03 Feb 2026 -- Israeli forces and settlers carried out 1,872 attacks against Palestinians and their property across the occupied West Bank in January, a new report reveals, highlighting a sharp escalation in violence, displacement, and settlement activity.

  • Hezbollah leader warns of 'existential threat' amid US-Israeli disarmament push Press TV 03 Feb 2026 -- Hezbollah Secretary-General Sheikh Naim Qassem has warned that a US-Israeli push to disarm the resistance in Lebanon is a calculated prelude to the annihilation of the Lebanese nation.

  • Secretary-General's remarks at the 2026 Opening Session of the Committee on the Exercise of the Inalienable Rights of the Palestinian People UN Secretary-General 03 Feb 2026
  • Rafah reopening offers lifeline for patients, but aid flow still far from enough UN News 03 Feb 2026 -- The partial reopening of the Rafah crossing between Gaza and Egypt has allowed a small number of people to leave and return to the Strip - including critically ill patients - but the UN has warned that far more is needed for the crossing to function as a genuine humanitarian lifeline.
  • Renewed calls for two-State solution amid 'perilously fragile' conditions for Palestinians UN News 03 Feb 2026 -- The UN committee mandated to promote Palestinian rights opened its 2026 session on Tuesday with renewed calls for a two-State solution, amid a shaky ceasefire in Gaza and continued settler expansion in the West Bank.
  • UN Chief warns Israel's E1 settlement plans threaten Palestinian statehood Press TV 03 Feb 2026 -- UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres has warned that Israel's "unlawful" plans to expand settlements in the E1 area of the West Bank could sever its northern and southern parts, dealing a serious blow to Palestinian statehood.

  • MSF chief warns Israeli ban on Gaza operations will be 'catastrophic' for millions Press TV 03 Feb 2026 -- The Israeli regime's decision to terminate Doctors Without Borders' humanitarian operations will trigger a "catastrophic" collapse in medical care for the people of Gaza, the charity's chief warned Monday.

  • The OIC Media Observatory on Israeli Crimes Against Palestinians Presents its 114th Report Amid Israel's Return to the Peak of Massacres: 52 Palestinian Martyrs in Gaza Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 03 Feb 2026 -- The OIC Media Observatory on Israeli Crimes Against Palestinians documented the deaths of 52 Palestinians in the Gaza Strip, in addition to the recovery of three bodies, the confirmation of the deaths of 85 others, and the recording of 136 injuries
  • Partial reopening of the Rafah crossing (February 3, 2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 03 Feb 2026 -- France welcomes the partial reopening of the Rafah border crossing in both directions, allowing for the first medical evacuations.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, South Sudan, Sudan, Ukraine UNOCHA 03 Feb 2026

  • Undermining PH, backing China in WPS is treason - PCG Philippine News Agency 03 Feb 2026 -- The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) on Tuesday said supporting China's claims in the West Philippine Sea (WPS) while undermining the Philippines' claims is "treason."
  • Malacañang says Senate WPS debate part of democracy, but PH claim firm Philippine News Agency 03 Feb 2026 -- Malacañang on Tuesday said the recent heated debate on the West Philippine Sea (WPS) in the Senate reflects the Philippines' democratic processes, but underscored that the administration remains firm on defending the country's maritime claims.
  • 19K ships monitored in PH waters last January Philippine News Agency 03 Feb 2026 -- The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP), along with the Philippine Navy (PN), monitored a total of 19,655 local and foreign ships all over the country's waters last January, a ranking naval official said on Tuesday.
  • PH, treaty ally to hold naval, air drills in WPS Philippine News Agency 03 Feb 2026 -- A ranking Philippine Navy (PN) official on Tuesday confirmed that the country will be conducting a series of naval and air exercises with a still unidentified treaty ally near Bajo de Masinloc, West Philippine Sea (WPS) from Jan. 21 to March 31 this year.

  • UNICEF statement on reported death and injury of children during attacks in Balochistan, Pakistan UNICEF 03 Feb 2026 -- Statement by UNICEF Representative in Pakistan, Pernille Ironside
  • Security Council Press Statement on Terrorist Attacks in Pakistan UN Security Council 03 Feb 2026
  • China will, as always, resolutely support Pakistan in combating terrorism: FM on severe terrorist attacks in Pakistan's Balochistan province Global Times 03 Feb 2026 -- When asked to comment on recent statements by the Pakistani military saying that the terrorist organization Balochistan Liberation Army had launched coordinated attacks in Quetta, Gwadar Port and other locations in Balochistan Province, killing 15 security personnel and more than 18 civilians as well as claims by the group that the attacks had resulted in the deaths of 84 Pakistani security personnel, Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said on Tuesday that China strongly condemns the attacks, expresses deep condolences to the victims, and extends sincere sympathies to the injured and the bereaved families.

  • In Washington, Fletcher presses for action as Sudan war grinds on UN News 03 Feb 2026 -- Sudan is now the world's most severe humanitarian emergency, the UN's top relief official warned on Tuesday, urging donors and diplomatic partners to act swiftly as the fighting nears a third year with no end in sight.
  • UN Relief Chief calls for truce, protection as Sudan's suffering deepens UNOCHA 03 Feb 2026 -- Remarks by UN Under-Secretary-General and Emergency Relief Coordinator, Tom Fletcher, at event to catalyze new funding contributions to the United Nations-led humanitarian response in Sudan

  • Lives of malnourished children at risk with upsurge in violence in South Sudan UNICEF 03 Feb 2026 -- More than 450,000 children are at risk of acute malnutrition as the hostilities are causing mass displacement and halting of critical health and nutrition services in Jonglei state, warned UNICEF in South Sudan.

News Reports

  • Chinese National Extradited from Guatemala to Face Charges of Narcotics Trafficking, Money Laundering and Material Support to Terrorism US Dept. of Justice 03 Feb 2026 -- Wenshen Xu, a Chinese national, was extradited from Guatemala to the United States yesterday to face an indictment returned in the Eastern District of Virginia charging him with conspiring to import cocaine into the United States, launder money derived from drug trafficking and provide material support to a designated Foreign Terrorist Organization, specifically the Cártel de Jalisco Nueva Generación(CJNG).

  • HRH the Crown Prince Chairs Cabinet Session in Riyadh SPA 03 Feb 2026 -- His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, chaired the Cabinet session held in Riyadh today
  • HRH the Crown Prince Holds Official Talks with Turkish President SPA 03 Feb 2026 -- His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, received this evening in Riyadh President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan of the Republic of Türkiye
  • HRH the Crown Prince Holds Phone Call with Russian President SPA 03 Feb 2026 -- His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, held a phone call today with President Vladimir Putin of the Russian Federation
  • Telephone conversation with Crown Prince and Prime Minister of Saudi Arabia Mohammed bin Salman Al Saud President of Russia 03 Feb 2026 -- Vladimir Putin had a telephone conversation with Crown Prince and Prime Minister of Saudi Arabia Mohammed bin Salman Al Saud.
  • Putin Holds Phone Talks With Saudi Crown Prince - Kremlin Sputnik 03 Feb 2026 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin held a telephone conversation with Saudi Arabia's Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman Al Saud, the Kremlin said on Tuesday.

  • India has made no official move to halt Russian oil imports: Kremlin Press TV 03 Feb 2026 -- The Kremlin says it has received no official statement from India about halting purchases of Russian oil, despite US President Donald Trump's claim that New Delhi would stop buying the country's crude as part of a deal with Washington
  • Wider Europe Briefing: Stopping Former Russian Soldiers From Entering The EU RFE/RL 03 Feb 2026

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian's Regular Press Conference on February 3, 2026 PRC MofA 03 Feb 2026
  • China's position not changed on keeping global industrial and supply chains of critical minerals safe and stable: FM on US' plan for strategic stockpile of critical minerals Global Times 03 Feb 2026 -- Reuters reported that US President Donald Trump announced plans on Monday to launch a strategic stockpile of critical minerals backed by $10 billion in seed funding from the US Export-Import Bank. In response to a request for comment on the fact that the US move comes at a time when Washington is seeking to reduce its dependence on China for rare earths and other resources, China's FM spokesperson Lin Jian told a press conference on Tuesday that China's position has not changed on keeping the global industrial and supply chains of critical minerals safe and stable. All parties have the responsibility to play a constructive role in this regard, added Lin.
  • Xi calls for cooperation with Uruguay in multiple areas PRC MND 03 Feb 2026 -- China and Uruguay should strengthen the alignment of development strategies, and deepen cooperation in areas such as economy and trade, finance, agriculture and animal husbandry, infrastructure construction, and information and communications technology, Chinese President Xi Jinping said on Tuesday.
  • President Xi Jinping Holds Talks With Uruguayan President Yamandú Orsi PRC MofA 03 Feb 2026 -- On the morning of February 3, 2026, President Xi Jinping held talks with President Yamandú Orsi of Uruguay, who is on a state visit to China, at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing.

  • KMT pushes back at U.S. senator's criticism of defense budget stance CNA 03 Feb 2026 -- A senior U.S. senator said he was "disappointed" that Taiwan's opposition parties have sought to "slash" a Cabinet-proposed defense budget, prompting the Kuomintang (KMT) to criticize his comments as based on "insufficient information."
  • Taiwan-U.S. relations will decide Taiwan's future: FM CNA 03 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Minister Lin Chia-lung (林佳龍) on Tuesday said Taiwan's relationship with the United States is of paramount importance to the country's future development despite the lack of official ties.
  • MAC criticizes KMT's participation in Beijing forum with CCP CNA 03 Feb 2026 -- The Mainland Affairs Council (MAC) on Tuesday accused the Kuomintang (KMT) of "ignoring growing hostility" after it joined the Chinese Communist Party (CCP) in expressing opposition to an independent Taiwan during a forum in Beijing.
  • Think tank forum co-hosted by CPC, KMT held in Beijing People's Daily 03 Feb 2026 -- A think tank forum co-hosted by research institutes affiliated with the Communist Party of China (CPC) and the Chinese Kuomintang (KMT) party was held in Beijing on Tuesday.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 03 Feb 2026

  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un's Speech at Inauguration Ceremony of Samgwang Livestock Farm KCNA 03 Feb 2026 -- The respected Comrade Kim Jong Un made a speech at the inauguration ceremony of the Samgwang Livestock Farm.

  • Iranian surveillance drone successfully completes mission in intl. waters amid reports of US sabotage Press TV 03 Feb 2026 -- An Iranian Armed Forces' patrol and surveillance drone has successfully completed its mission in international waters amid reports of United States forces' downing one such aircraft.
  • Iranian Admiral Warns US Attack Would Trigger Strike on Israel Sputnik 03 Feb 2026 -- Ali Shamkhani, a senior advisor to Iran's Supreme Leader has declared that Tehran views the US and Israel as a single military entity.
  • Hegseth: US 'Prepared' To Take Action Against Iran RFE/RL 03 Feb 2026 -- US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has once again warned Tehran that the Pentagon is "more than prepared" to take action if Iran refuses to negotiate over its nuclear program.
  • Witkoff In Middle East For Talks Aimed At Making Progress On Nuclear Deal With Iran RFE/RL 03 Feb 2026 -- US envoy Steve Witkoff met with Israeli officials for more than three hours on February 3, two days before a key meeting in Istanbul with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi in an attempt to jumpstart negotiations on Tehran's nuclear program and tamp down fears of a new war in the Middle East.
  • Potential U.S.-Iran nuclear talks face mixed signals amid rising tensions People's Daily 03 Feb 2026 -- Plans for potential U.S.-Iran nuclear negotiations remain uncertain as reports of an upcoming meeting in Istanbul were questioned by Iranian officials, while both sides continue to exchange warnings and bolster military deployments amid rising regional tensions.
  • Iran finalizes planning for talks with US; venue to be announced: Foreign Ministry Press TV 03 Feb 2026 -- The Foreign Ministry says preparations have been completed for holding talks with the United States in the coming days, with consultations continuing on the location of the negotiations.
  • 'No threats, no pressure': President Pezeshkian lays down terms for talks with US Press TV 03 Feb 2026 -- Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian says he has instructed the country's foreign minister to pursue negotiations with the United States, provided they take place in a respectful and threat-free environment.
  • Iran's foreign minister ramps up regional diplomacy amid tensions with US Press TV 03 Feb 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has launched a flurry of high-level diplomatic consultations, holding phone talks with his counterparts in Turkey, Qatar, Oman, and Kuwait.
  • Israel pressing for Iran strike, Trump remains reluctant: Report Press TV 03 Feb 2026 -- The Israeli regime has been pressing US President Donald Trump to authorize a strike on Iran, but Trump has remained reluctant, according to a report citing statements from US officials.
  • Iran's Network Of Proxies 'Activated' As Possible War With US Looms RFE/RL 03 Feb 2026 -- When Israel and the United States conducted an intensive bombing campaign in Iran last year, Tehran's regional network of armed proxies and partners largely stood on the sidelines.
  • EU labeling IRGC in line with US-Israeli harassing actions: Iran IRNA 03 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei says Iran has reacted harshly at all levels to the European Union resolution against the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC), arguing that Europe's move was in line with hostile actions and intimidation of the United States and the Israeli regime.
  • Iran summons EU ambassadors over 'provocative' IRGC designation Press TV 03 Feb 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Ministry has summoned ambassadors of European Union member states in protest against a recent EU resolution against the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC).
  • Iran Summons EU Ambassadors Over IRGC's Terrorist Labeling - Foreign Ministry Sputnik 03 Feb 2026 -- Iran has summoned ambassadors of EU member states in response to the European Union's decision to list the Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organization, the Iranian Foreign Ministry said on Tuesday.
  • Hezbollah chief says Iran can defeat US and Israel in potential conflict IRNA 03 Feb 2026 -- Hezbollah's secretary general says Iran is capable of defeating the United States and Israel in any potential confrontation amid mounting tensions in the region caused by the US dispatch of its warships.
  • Pakistan invited to attend US-Iran talks in Turkiye: Foreign Ministry IRNA 03 Feb 2026 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Pakistan has confirmed that Islamabad has been invited to participate in talks between Iran and the US, which are set to take place in Istanbul, Turkiye, later this week.
  • Qatar: High-level dialogue with Iran ongoing to prevent regional escalation Press TV 03 Feb 2026 -- Qatar says it is continuing high-level political consultations with Iran and other regional actors in an effort to prevent further escalation of tensions amid warmongering by the United States.
  • Russian FM slams US threats against Iran as 'unacceptable' as Moscow pushes for de-escalation Press TV 03 Feb 2026 -- Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov has condemned new US threats of military action against Iran, warning that any renewed act of aggression would have grave regional and global consequences.
  • Pezeshkian emphasizes development and strengthening of Iran's space industry IRNA 03 Feb 2026 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian has called for the need to develop and strategically boost the space industry through homegrown technology, emphasizing the importance of this sector in the country's macro-development.
  • Iran unveils three homegrown strategic projects on National Space Technology Day Press TV 03 Feb 2026 -- Iran has unveiled and inaugurated three domestically developed and strategic achievements in the fields of infrastructure, communications, and satellite imagery as it commemorates National Space Technology Day.

  • COMBAT TERRORISM THROUGH CAPACITY BUILDING PROGRAMME India PIB 03 Feb 2026 -- As part of India's growing leadership in the global fight against terror financing, the Department of Revenue, in collaboration with the Ministry of External Affairs and the National Security Council Secretariat, organized a first-of-its-kind Capacity Building Programme for representatives of the Central Asian Republics (CARs) on "Countering the Financing of Terrorism (CFT) through Cryptocurrencies, Crowdfunding, and Non-Profit Organizations" on 21st-22nd April 2025 in New Delhi

  • A high-level Libyan delegation called on Prime Minister Muhammad Shehbaz Sharif at Prime Minister's House, today. Pakistan - Prime Miinister's Office 03 Feb 2026

  • 20 Persons and 3 entities listed under the Autonomous Sanctions Regulations 2011 - Iran Australia DFAT 03 Feb 2026

  • Israel and Azerbaijan sign MOU on Artificial Intelligence Israel - Prime Minister 03 Feb 2026 -- Israel and Azerbaijan signed today , at the Prime Minister's Office in Jerusalem, a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) in the field of Artificial Intelligence.

  • Japan's remilitarization threatens Asia-Pacific stability: Lavrov People's Daily 03 Feb 2026 -- Japan's policy of accelerated remilitarization threatens peace and stability in the Asia-Pacific region, Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov warned on Monday.

  • The Daily Press Briefing (02/03/2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 03 Feb 2026
  • France adopts 2026 state budget after gov't survives no-confidence votes People's Daily 03 Feb 2026 -- France adopted its 2026 state budget after the government led by Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu survived two no-confidence votes over the issue in the National Assembly on Monday.

  • New Zealand and Germany strengthen partnership New Zealand Government 03 Feb 2026 -- New Zealand and Germany have agreed to strengthen their strategic partnership, Foreign Minister Winston Peters has announced.
  • Joint statement by New Zealand Foreign Minister Winston Peters and German Federal Foreign Minister H.E. Wadephul New Zealand Government 03 Feb 2026
  • Joint statement on Antarctic Cooperation between the Government of the Federal Republic of Germany and the Government of New Zealand New Zealand Government 03 Feb 2026

  • Arctic State of Affairs: Keynote speech by HR/VP Kaja Kallas at the 2026 Arctic Frontiers Conference European External Action Service (EEAS) 03 Feb 2026
  • Arctic Frontiers Conference: Remarks by the High Representative Kaja Kallas at the Joint press conference with Espen Barth Eide, Minister of Foreign Affairs of Norway European External Action Service (EEAS) 03 Feb 2026

  • The Moroccan Ministry of Foreign Affairs, African Cooperation and Moroccan Expatriates and the United States Department of State are honored to announce the second plenary meeting of the Global Forum to Prevent Radiological and Nuclear (R/N) Terrorism (Global FTPRNT), to be held from February 3-5, 2026, in Rabat, Morocco. Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs 03 Feb 2026

  • WHO launches 2026 appeal to help millions of people in health emergencies and crisis settings WHO 03 Feb 2026
  • Building healthy bridges towards peace: WHO launches $1 billion appeal UN News 03 Feb 2026 -- The World Health Organization (WHO) on Tuesday launched its 2026 global appeal for nearly $1 billion to ensure that millions of people living in humanitarian crises and conflicts can access healthcare.

  • World News in Brief: Children at risk in South Sudan, Balochistan attacks, summit backs boost for undersea cable security UN News 03 Feb 2026 -- More than 450,000 children in South Sudan are at risk of acute malnutrition as a surge in violence disrupts health and nutrition services, the UN children's agency (UNICEF) warned on Tuesday.
